name: The First National Bank Of Litchfield
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank offering consumer, real estate, commercial, and agricultural loans with local loan servicing and online/mobile banking.
description_long:
First National Bank of Litchfield is a traditional community bank that emphasizes local service and relationship banking. The bank is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing deposit safety for customers. They operate physical branches and maintain ATM networks while offering digital banking options.

The bank provides a comprehensive suite of lending products across multiple categories. Consumer loans cover auto purchases, vacations, and debt consolidation. Real estate offerings include fixed-rate mortgages, adjustable-rate mortgages (ARMs), construction loans, and USDA Rural Housing Program financing with 100% LTV options for qualified borrowers. Commercial lending supports business startups, expansions, and equipment financing with flexible repayment structures. Agricultural lending is substantial, including operating lines of credit, machinery financing, livestock loans, farm real estate, and beginning farmer programs in partnership with the Illinois Finance Authority and Farm Services Agency. The bank also offers CDs and IRAs for savings.

Key distinguishing features include local loan servicing—the bank retains mortgage servicing rights rather than selling loans, meaning payments stay local. They offer Freddie Mac mortgage products and specifically market to first-time homebuyers and rural properties. Agricultural borrowers can contact named loan officers (Ken Elmore and Kevin Niemann) for specialized farm lending. Digital options include online banking, mobile banking apps, and paperless statement enrollment.

As a community bank, First National Bank of Litchfield likely has limited geographic reach and may have fewer technology features than larger national banks. Specific interest rates, fees, and loan terms are not disclosed on their website. The bank appears well-established but provides minimal information about minimum deposits, account requirements, or specific CD rates, which is typical for community banks that require direct contact for rate quotes.
services: Consumer loans for auto purchases, vacations, and debt consolidation
Fixed-rate mortgage loans with Freddie Mac products
Construction loans with interest-only payments during build phase
USDA Rural Housing Program financing with 100% LTV guarantee
Commercial loans for business startups, expansion, and equipment
Machinery and equipment financing for farm operations
Livestock financing for feeder and breeding stock
Farm real estate purchase and refinancing
Beginning farmer loans through Illinois Finance Authority partnership
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Local loan servicing on mortgages—payments stay with the bank rather than being sold
USDA Rural Housing Program financing available with 100% LTV for qualified borrowers
Specialized agricultural lending with named loan officers and programs for beginning farmers
Flexible construction loan terms with interest-only payments during building phase
Freddie Mac mortgage products for fixed and adjustable-rate financing
Online banking, mobile app, and paperless statements available for convenience
cons: No interest rates, APRs, or specific loan terms disclosed on website
Limited information about account minimums, monthly fees, or CD rates
Community bank likely has limited geographic branch network
Website lacks details on loan approval timelines or application process
Agricultural lending requires personal contact with specific officers rather than online application
best_for: Rural homebuyers seeking USDA Rural Housing Program financing with local servicing
Agricultural operators needing specialized farm lending from officers with industry knowledge
First-time homebuyers preferring local banks with flexible mortgage terms
Small business owners in their service area seeking relationship-based commercial lending
diagnosis: First National Bank of Litchfield is best for rural borrowers, farmers, and first-time homebuyers in their service area who value local relationship banking and loan servicing. The main caveat is that detailed terms, rates, and account information are not available online—potential customers must contact the bank directly to compare offerings with competitors.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
